result.name: The rlogin service is running

nvt.name: The rlogin service is running
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:P|summary=This remote host is running a rlogin service.|insight=rlogin has several serious security problems,
- all information, including passwords, is transmitted unencrypted.
- .rlogin (or .rhosts) file is easy to misuse (potentially allowing anyone to login without a password)|affected=|impact=|solution=Disable the rlogin service and use alternatives like SSH instead.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Disable the rlogin service and use alternatives like SSH instead.

result.description: The rlogin service is running on the target system.

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (192.168.40.7) to target (192.168.40.3):
192.168.40.7 192.168.40.3
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (192.168.40.7) to target (192.168.40.3):
192.168.40.7 192.168.40.3
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: TWiki < 4.2.4 Multiple XSS / Command Execution Vulnerabilities

nvt.name: TWiki < 4.2.4 Multiple XSS / Command Execution Vulnerabilities
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=TWiki is prone to multiple cross-site scripting (XSS) and command execution vulnerabilities.|insight=The flaws are due to:
- %URLPARAM{}% variable is not properly sanitized which lets attackers conduct cross-site scripting attack.
- %SEARCH{}% variable is not properly sanitised before being used in an eval() call which lets the attackers execute perl code through eval injection attack.|affected=TWiki versions prior to 4.2.4.|impact=Successful exploitation could allow execution of arbitrary script code or commands. This could let attackers steal cookie-based authentication credentials or compromise the affected application.|solution=Update to version 4.2.4 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: TWiki < 6.1.0 XSS Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=bin/statistics in TWiki 6.0.2 allows XSS via the webs parameter.|insight=|affected=TWiki version 6.0.2 and probably prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 6.1.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.name: TWiki Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability (Sep 2010)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:P|summary=TWiki is prone to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.|insight=Attack can be done by tricking an authenticated TWiki user into visiting a static HTML page on another side, where a Javascript enabled browser will send an HTTP POST request to TWiki, which in turn will process the request as the TWiki user.|affected=TWiki version prior to 4.3.2|impact=Successful exploitation will allow attacker to gain administrative privileges on the target application and can cause CSRF attack.|solution=Upgrade to TWiki version 4.3.2 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to TWiki version 4.3.2 or later.

nvt.name: TWiki CSRF Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:S/C:P/I:P/A:P|summary=TWiki is prone to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.|insight=Remote authenticated user can create a specially crafted image tag that, when viewed by the target user, will update pages on the target system with the privileges of the target user via HTTP requests.|affected=TWiki version prior to 4.3.1|impact=Successful exploitation will allow attacker to gain administrative privileges on the target application and can cause CSRF attack.|solution=Upgrade to version 4.3.1 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: TWiki Version Detection

nvt.name: TWiki Version Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Detection of TWiki.
The script sends a HTTP connection request to the server and attempts to detect the presence of TWiki and to extract its version.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: Detected TWiki Version: 01.Feb.2003 Location: /twiki/bin CPE: cpe:/a:twiki:twiki:01.Feb.2003 Concluded from version/product identification result: This site is running TWiki version 01 Feb 2003

result.name: VNC Brute Force Login

nvt.name: VNC Brute Force Login
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:P/A:P|summary=Try to log in with given passwords via VNC protocol.|insight=This script tries to authenticate to a VNC server with the passwords set in the password preference. It will also test and report if no authentication / password is required at all.
Note: Some VNC servers have a blacklisting scheme that blocks IP addresses after five unsuccessful connection attempts for a period of time. The script will abort the brute force attack if it encounters that it gets blocked.
Note as well that passwords can be max. 8 characters long.|affected=|impact=|solution=Change the password to something hard to guess or enable password protection at all.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Change the password to something hard to guess or enable password protection at all.

result.description: It was possible to connect to the VNC server with the password: password

result.name: VNC Server and Protocol Version Detection (TCP)

nvt.name: VNC Server and Protocol Version Detection (TCP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=TCP based detection of Virtual Network Computing (VNC) services and the underlying Remote Framebuffer Protocol (RFB) version.|insight=The remote host is running a remote display software (VNC) which permits a console to be displayed remotely.
This allows authenticated users of the remote host to take its control remotely.|affected=|impact=|solution=Make sure the use of this software is done in accordance with your corporate security policy, filter incoming traffic to this port.|vuldetect=|solution_type=
nvt.solution: Make sure the use of this software is done in accordance with your corporate security policy, filter incoming traffic to this port.

result.description:
A VNC server seems to be running on this port.
The version of the VNC protocol is : RFB 003.003
